target.name: NetworkOcean
target.founder: Sam Mendel (CEO; serial founder, prior exit of a creator-hiring marketplace), Eric Kim (CTO; ex-Palantir, Merge; Cornell)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $1M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
NetworkOcean is a Seed-stage ocean and maritime technology company founded by Sam Mendel (serial entrepreneur with a prior marketplace exit) and Eric Kim (former Palantir and Merge engineer). The company has raised $1M to date. The private comp universe for ocean tech includes three relevant peers: Standard Subsea, Clippership, and Albacore. No direct public comparables exist in this subsector.

Given the early stage and limited data availability, a single valuation method was applied: stage-progression analysis. This method uses the $1M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $2M. This approach is appropriate for frontier-tech companies with limited operating history but credible founding teams.

The triangulated valuation range is $2.0M (low) to $3.0M (high), with a median of $2M. The narrow range reflects reliance on a single applicable method and the absence of public market or revenue-based anchors. Key uncertainties include actual traction metrics (revenue, user adoption, or product development milestones not provided in available data), the relevance and stage of the three private comparables, and sector-specific dynamics in ocean tech. A more robust analysis would benefit from revenue-based or transaction multiples once the company reaches a later stage with clearer performance visibility.
